records.ety: [AS. forsacan to oppose, refuse; for- + sacan to contend, strive; akin to Goth. sakan. See For-, and Sake.]
records.morph: [imp. Forsook (?); p. p. Forsaken (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Forsaking.]

defs: To quit or leave entirely; to desert; to abandon; to depart or withdraw from; to leave; as, false friends and flatterers forsake us in adversity.
quotes: If his children forsake my law, and walk not in my judgments.

defs: To renounce; to reject; to refuse.
quotes: If you forsake the offer of their love.
